title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get creamy, rich, and flavorful Indian Butter Chicken at home!
longTailKeywords: Butter Chicken recipe step by step
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani. Perfect for an Indian lunch or dinner!

description: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian classic, featuring tender chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This iconic dish is celebrated for its harmonious blend of tangy tomatoes, aromatic spices, and a luxurious touch of butter and cream. It's the perfect comforting meal, offering a symphony of flavors that will transport your taste buds straight to India.

ingredients.notes: divided, for color and mild heat
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, garam masala, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt. Mix thoroughly to coat the chicken. Marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or for 2-4 hours in the refrigerator for best results.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter or ghee in a large heavy-bottomed pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until the chicken is lightly browned on all sides and mostly cooked through. Remove the cooked chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter or ghee. Once melted, add the finely chopped onions and sauté over medium heat until they turn translucent and soft, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, remaining 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, and 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder. Add a teaspoon of salt. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. Drain the soaked cashews, blend them with a little water to form a smooth paste, and stir this cashew paste into the simmering sauce. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil begins to separate from the edges.

instructions.title: Blend and Strain the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the hot sauce to a blender. Blend until it is completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky texture, strain the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan, pressing down on any solids. This step is optional but highly recommended for restaurant-quality butter chicken.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish the Dish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the smooth, strained sauce. Add the crushed kasoori methi, green chilies (if using), and sugar (if desired to balance the tanginess of the tomatoes). Stir in the heavy cream. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to finish cooking through in the rich sauce. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani from heat. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro and a final swirl of heavy cream, if desired. Serve hot with warm naan bread, roti, or fluffy basmati rice for a truly authentic Indian meal.

tips: For the best flavor, marinate the chicken overnight. The longer it marinates, the more tender and flavorful your chicken will be.
Using Kashmiri red chili powder is key to achieving the signature vibrant red color of butter chicken without adding excessive heat.
To achieve a truly silky smooth restaurant-style sauce, blend the tomato-onion base until no chunks remain, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve.
article:
Unlock the secrets to a truly unforgettable Indian culinary experience right in your own kitchen with the iconic Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani. This universally beloved dish, renowned for its rich, creamy texture and subtly spiced tomato gravy, is a staple among Indian lunch recipes and a testament to the country's vibrant food culture. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook eager to explore new flavors, mastering Butter Chicken promises a rewarding journey into the heart of authentic Indian cuisine. Get ready to transform simple ingredients into a gourmet feast that will tantalize your taste buds and impress everyone at your table.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s, this dish was famously created at the Moti Mahal restaurant. It quickly rose to prominence due to its unique combination of smoky tandoor-cooked chicken (or pan-fried chicken) immersed in a luscious, velvety gravy. The sauce is a harmonious blend of pureed tomatoes, cashews, cream, and a medley of aromatic spices like ginger, garlic, garam masala, and fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). Unlike some spicier Indian curries, Butter Chicken is celebrated for its mild, sweet, and tangy profile, making it a crowd-plepleaser across all age groups and a perfect centerpiece for any Indian lunch. Its vibrant color and comforting richness make it an irresistible choice for those seeking a taste of authentic Indian comfort food.

## Ingredients You'll Need

To embark on your Butter Chicken journey, gather these essential ingredients. Fresh, quality components are key to an exceptional flavor.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch cubes
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, optional)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1/2 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp salt
*   1 tbsp lemon juice

For the Makhani Gravy:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter (plus extra for finishing)
*   1 tbsp oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 medium fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews, soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes
*   1/2 tsp red chili powder
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1 tsp sugar
*   1/2 tsp salt (or to taste)
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1/4 cup heavy cream (or more, to taste)
*   1 tsp kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed

For Garnish:
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   A swirl of heavy cream

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to create a magnificent Butter Chicken:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary, to avoid overcrowding the pan. Cook for 5-7 minutes on each side, until beautifully browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan, melt 2 tbsp butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent and softened, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Aromatics and Tomatoes:** Add the ginger-garlic paste to the pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in the crushed tomatoes or fresh tomato puree. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have reduced and darkened slightly.
5.  **Add Spices and Cashews:** Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the pan along with red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, sugar, and salt. Mix well and cook for another 2-3 minutes.
6.  **Blend the Gravy:** Carefully transfer the entire mixture from the pan to a blender. Add 1/2 cup water or chicken broth. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky texture, you can strain the gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the clean pan.
7.  **Simmer and Finish:** Return the smooth gravy to the pan. Bring it to a gentle simmer. Add the cooked chicken pieces. Stir well to coat the chicken.
8.  **Cream and Kasoori Methi:** Reduce the heat to low. Stir in the heavy cream and crushed kasoori methi. Cook for another 3-5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed.
9.  **Garnish and Serve:** Ladle the hot Butter Chicken into serving bowls. Garnish with fresh cilantro and an optional swirl of extra cream or a small pat of butter.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal for the best Butter Chicken.
*   **Quality Tomatoes:** Use ripe, flavorful fresh tomatoes or good quality canned crushed tomatoes. They form the backbone of the gravy.
*   **Smooth Gravy:** Blending the gravy until absolutely smooth is crucial for that signature creamy texture. Straining it afterwards takes it to another level of silky perfection.
*   **Toasted Cashews (Optional):** Lightly toasting the cashews before soaking and blending can add a deeper nutty flavor to the gravy.
*   **Kasoori Methi:** Don't skip the kasoori methi. Rub it between your palms before adding it to release its aromatic oils; it imparts a unique, slightly bitter, and herbaceous flavor that is essential to authentic Murgh Makhani.
*   **Dhungar Method (Smoky Flavor):** For an authentic smoky flavor reminiscent of tandoor cooking, place a small piece of burning charcoal in a small heatproof bowl inside the pot of Butter Chicken. Drizzle a teaspoon of ghee or oil over the charcoal, cover immediately, and let it smoke for 2-3 minutes. Remove the charcoal before serving.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments.
*   **Breads:** Warm naan bread (garlic naan is especially good), roti, or paratha are perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera (cumin) rice complements the creamy curry wonderfully.
*   **Sides:** A simple cucumber raita (yogurt with cucumber) or a fresh green salad can provide a refreshing contrast to the richness of the Butter Chicken.

## Variations and Substitutions

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables (potatoes, peas, carrots) to create Paneer Makhani or a delicious vegetable makhani.
*   **Spice Level:** For more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili during the gravy preparation. For less heat, reduce or omit chili powder.
*   **Dairy-Free:** Substitute yogurt with a dairy-free alternative like coconut yogurt for marination. Use full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of heavy cream for the gravy. Ensure your butter substitute is also dairy-free.
*   **Nut-Free:** If you have a nut allergy, omit cashews and use sunflower seeds, poppy seeds, or even a small amount of roasted gram flour (besan) as a thickener, adjusting consistency with water or broth.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

**Q: Is Butter Chicken spicy?**
A: Traditional Butter Chicken is generally mild with a hint of sweetness and tang. While it contains chili powder, it's primarily for color and a subtle warmth rather than intense heat. You can adjust the spice level to your preference.

**Q: Can I make Butter Chicken ahead of time?**
A: Absolutely! Butter Chicken often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld. Prepare the dish completely, let it cool, then refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or cream if needed to restore consistency.

**Q: How do I store leftovers?**
A: Store leftover Butter Chicken in an airtight container in the refrigerator for 3-4 days. It can also be frozen for up to 2-3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.

**Q: What's the difference between Butter Chicken and Chicken Tikka Masala?**
A: While both are creamy, tomato-based dishes with marinated chicken, there are key differences. Butter Chicken typically has a sweeter, richer, and milder tomato-cream sauce, often with cashews. Chicken Tikka Masala tends to be spicier, with a more pronounced onion and bell pepper base, and a slightly tangier, less sweet profile. The marination for Chicken Tikka often involves more spices and a charring process similar to tandoori cooking.

**Q: Can I use chicken breasts instead of thighs?**
A: Yes, you can use chicken breasts. However, chicken thighs are often preferred for Butter Chicken because they tend to remain more tender and moist during cooking, absorbing the flavors better. If using breasts, be careful not to overcook them to prevent dryness.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a dish; it's an experience. Its comforting warmth and exquisite flavors make it a perfect choice for an Indian lunch recipe that promises satisfaction. With this detailed guide, you're now equipped to recreate this classic masterpiece in your own kitchen. Enjoy the rich aromas and incredible taste of your homemade Butter Chicken!
